data.basisOfEstimate: This is a formal RFP (Best Value evaluation) for an email marketing platform replacement for the California State Lottery. NO government estimated value is provided in the solicitation. The organization's labor rates (Manufacturing Engineer $178.89/hr, QA $16.86/hr, Software Engineer $171.40/hr) are completely irrelevant because this solicitation requires an existing commercial email marketing SaaS platform — not custom software development. Horus Technology is an AWS consulting firm specializing in document automation and AI/ML, which is a fundamentally different industry than enterprise email marketing platforms. The solicitation requires proposers to have an existing email platform product capable of 10M+ contacts, 1B+ annual sends, and 200+ automated workflows, with 5+ years of operational history. Competitors for this contract would be established email marketing platforms such as Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze, Iterable, MessageGears, or similar. Total price is set to $0 because the company fundamentally cannot perform this work as a prime contractor. Price confidence is set to 12 (below 15) because this is a formal solicitation but the company has zero capability alignment, no relevant past performance, and no basis to estimate a competitive price for a product it does not possess.
assumptions: Horus Technology does not own or operate an email marketing platform product
The company's past performance is in AWS cloud infrastructure, document automation, and AI/ML — not email marketing SaaS
The mandatory qualifications (5+ years providing email platform services) are disqualifying for Horus Technology
Organization labor rates provided are for software engineering roles, not email platform licensing or SaaS delivery
Competitors will be established email marketing platform vendors with existing products and government references
The 5% Small Business preference (up to $50,000) would not overcome the fundamental capability gap
The evaluation weights are: Technical 70% (500 points qualifications + NTR, 200 points demonstrations) and Cost 30% (300 points) — technical disqualification would occur before cost evaluation
pricingRisks: Fundamental industry mismatch: Horus Technology specializes in AWS cloud-native development, document automation, and AI/ML solutions — NOT email marketing platform services
Mandatory qualifications require 5+ years of experience providing an email service platform to organizations of similar size and scope — Horus Technology does not have this
Platform must handle 10M+ audience file, 1B+ annual email sends, and 200+ workflows — requires an established email marketing SaaS product, not custom development
Proposer must have 5+ years working with governmental entities or large public sector organizations on email platforms specifically
No government estimated value provided in the solicitation, making competitive pricing impossible to calibrate
Fixed-price contract type places all cost risk on the contractor for a platform they do not possess

Cost is weighted at 30% (300 of 1000 points) with lowest cost receiving maximum points — competing against established email platform vendors (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze, Iterable) who have economies of scale
Background investigation requirements and California-specific compliance add administrative burden
Demonstrations may be required (200 points) — cannot demonstrate a product that does not exist
recommendedActions: NO-BID RECOMMENDED: This solicitation requires an established email marketing platform vendor with 5+ years of proven platform delivery — Horus Technology does not have a qualifying product or relevant past performance
The mandatory qualifications (PQ1-PQ15) are disqualifying — Horus Technology cannot meet the 5-year experience requirements for email platform delivery
If pursuing tangentially, consider partnering as a subcontractor to a prime email platform vendor for data migration or integration services leveraging AWS expertise
Monitor future California State Lottery IT solicitations that align with cloud infrastructure, document automation, or AI/ML capabilities
Leverage California DGS Small Business certification for future state opportunities that match core competencies

evaluationFactors: Proposer DQ (Desirable Qualifications) - Maximum 100 points (DQ 16: 25 pts, DQ 17: 25 pts, DQ 18: 25 pts, DQ 19: 25 pts)
NTR: Understanding and Approach - Maximum 200 points
NTR: Customer Service Strategy and Platform Integrity Plan - Maximum 100 points
Technical Requirements scored 70% and Cost scored 30% prior to preference programs and negotiations
Narrative Technical Response scored using Exceptional (100%), Meets (50%), Deficient (0%), Fail (Disqualified) criteria
Demonstrations evaluated on: Clarity of Presentation (20%), Adherence to Use Cases (30%), Functional Alignment (30%), Technical Capabilities (20%)
Best value determination: maximum benefits to the Lottery in areas of security, competence, experience, qualifications, performance, and price
Small Business Preference: 5% preference up to $50,000
DVBE Incentive: 1%-5% based on participation level, up to $100,000
data.overview: The California State Lottery seeks a qualified vendor to implement a replacement email marketing and campaign management platform, including implementation services, data migration, IP warming, testing, training, and ongoing customer support. The contract has a 2-year base term with up to three 1-year extensions. Success means delivering a more functional, robust, and advanced email platform that enables effective email marketing campaigns, improved player engagement, and optimized communications for millions of registered players.

requirements.requirement: Provide an email marketing and campaign management solution that is more functional, robust, and advanced than Adobe Campaign Standard
Provide implementation services including hands-on keyboard support, seamless integration, data migration, IP warming, testing, and overall platform setup
Support migration of large database, tables, workflows, and audience profiles from existing platform
Support configuration of multiple instances of the email platform
Provide dedicated implementation team (in-house or third-party) for implementation services
Provide user training and support after implementation

Platform must support email communications to millions of registered players including game information, news, product updates, winner news, responsible gaming tips, promotions, retailer surveys, and other communications
Provide Service Level Agreements (SLAs) for platform support and issue resolution
Provide dedicated account team or single point of contact for real-time support
Provide platform up-time guarantees and define how outages are communicated and addressed
SaaS must be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, excluding agreed-upon maintenance downtime
SaaS monthly availability must average at least 99.9% excluding agreed-upon maintenance downtime
Data must be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, excluding agreed-upon maintenance downtime with 99.9% monthly availability
Provide advance written notice of any major upgrades or changes that will affect SaaS availability
All sensitive and confidential electronic Lottery Data in transit or at rest must be encrypted in compliance with FIPS 140-3 Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
Data handling must comply with Lottery's Encryption Policy and Information Security policies and Information Security Program Manual
Disposal of sensitive and confidential Lottery Data must meet ISO/IEC 27001 and NIST 800-88 minimum requirements

Provide a Security Plan within 10 days after contract execution identifying administrative, physical, and technical security controls, aligned with ISO/IEC 27001, ISO/IEC 27002, NIST SP 800-53, FIPS 199, and FIPS 140-3
Submit updated Security Plans on an annual basis within 30 days after the anniversary of the Contract start date

Security Plan must include Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery plan, incident response plan, intrusion detection/prevention, vulnerability management, antivirus, patching, security training, and audit logging
Notify Lottery Contract Manager, Deputy Director of SLED, and Information Security Office within two hours after discovery of an Information Security Incident
All Lottery Data maintained by Contractor must be physically located within the continental United States
Remote access to Lottery Data from outside the United States is prohibited unless approved in advance in writing by the Lottery
Maintain data backup and recovery processes for all critical Lottery Data with Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) as specified by the Lottery
Contractor and personnel must submit to background investigations including fingerprinting and inquiry to California DOJ and FBI
Proposer must not be presently debarred, suspended, proposed for debarment, or declared ineligible by any state or federal agency
Agree to Lottery Special Terms and Conditions (Exhibit C) without deviations
Agree to Information Technology Terms and Conditions (Exhibit D)
Comply with Scope of Work (Exhibit A SOW) without deviations
Immediately disclose to Lottery if Contractor intends to provide or utilize previously undisclosed Generative AI as a deliverable or to complete any portion of Contract deliverables or services
Joint proposals are not permitted; only a single primary proposer may submit
Maintain all Contract-related financial and accounting records for four years after Contract expiration and make available for Lottery audit
Upon Contract expiration or termination, all Lottery Data must be returned or destroyed beyond recovery within 90 days at Contractor's cost
Proposer must have minimum of two (2) and maximum of four (4) contract/implementations demonstrating relevant experience within the last 10 years completed in the United States
Proposer must meet all Mandatory Qualification (MQ) requirements as specified in Attachment 10.1 Proposer Qualifications Form
Contractor or subcontractor personnel must not be assigned to work on the Lottery Contract if they have ever been convicted of a felony, gambling-related offense, or a crime involving dishonesty
Designate a single point of contact for background processing
Document any assumptions about Exhibit A SOW, responsibilities, and other issues relevant to proposal submission and ability to do the work for the proposed cost
Proposer's response constitutes an irrevocable offer for 180 calendar days following the proposal due date
Rates shall be inclusive of all direct and indirect costs for all activities, labor, materials, tools, equipment, and travel
Continue to provide goods and services pursuant to the Contract until migration to a replacement contract with a new contractor is complete upon termination or expiration

format: Must be in Microsoft Word 365; figures, tables, charts inserted into MS Word document (exception: Cost Sheet in MS Excel and PDF attachments)
Standard letter 8.5" x 11" size with fonts meeting ADA requirements (e.g., Arial 12 pt.)
Page numbers in same position throughout proposal
Each page must show: RFP R004429, Name of proposer, Volume number, Attachment Number, Page number (Page # of ##)
No cost information in any volume except Volume 3
Submit electronically through Lottery Secure File Transfer (Liquid Files) site
Each Volume must be submitted as its own separate Liquid Files submission
Subject line format: Proposer's Company name_RFP R004429_Volume X '# of ##'
Understanding and Approach narrative must not exceed 4 pages
Implementation Services narrative must not exceed 4 pages
Customer Service Strategy and Platform Integrity Plan narrative must not exceed 4 pages
Assumptions narrative must not exceed 2 pages
attachmentsAndForms: Attachment 1: Cover Letter on Company Letterhead
Attachment 7: Small and Microbusiness Participation (if claiming)
Attachment 12: GenAI Impact Assessment Form (if applicable)

requiredDocuments.name: Cover Letter
requiredDocuments.description: Cover letter on company letterhead containing legal company name, address, statement of appropriate skills, statement of compliance with Exhibit A SOW, authorized signature with name and title, and email/phone of signer

requiredDocuments.name: Solicitation Submission Checklist (Attachment 2)
requiredDocuments.description: Completed checklist confirming all required items are contained within the proposal

requiredDocuments.name: Response to Administrative Requirements (Attachment 4)
requiredDocuments.description: Proposer indicates agreement to each Administrative Requirement by marking Yes/No

requiredDocuments.name: Proposer Certification (Attachment 5)
requiredDocuments.description: Signed certification acknowledging compliance with all solicitation terms, qualifications, pricing independence, insurance/bond, and confidentiality requirements

requiredDocuments.name: Proposer's Preferences and Incentives (Attachment 6)
requiredDocuments.description: Indicate whether claiming Small Business Preference and/or DVBE Incentive

requiredDocuments.name: Small and Microbusiness Participation (Attachment 7)
requiredDocuments.description: Required if claiming Small Business Preference; includes declaration of compliance and subcontractor forms

requiredDocuments.name: DVBE Participation (Attachment 8)
requiredDocuments.description: Required if claiming DVBE Incentive; includes subcontractor and DVBE proposer forms with certification copies

requiredDocuments.name: Responsibility Certification (Attachment 9)
requiredDocuments.description: Signed certification that proposer and subcontractors are not presently debarred, suspended, or declared ineligible by any state or federal agency

requiredDocuments.name: Proposer Qualifications Form (Attachment 10.1)
requiredDocuments.description: Completed form documenting minimum 2 and maximum 4 contract/implementations demonstrating relevant experience within last 10 years in the United States, with verifiable references for MQ and DQ requirements

requiredDocuments.name: Narrative Technical Response (Attachment 11)
requiredDocuments.description: Narrative responses covering Understanding and Approach (max 4 pages), Implementation Services (max 4 pages), Customer Service Strategy and Platform Integrity Plan (max 4 pages), and Assumptions (max 2 pages)

requiredDocuments.name: GenAI Impact Assessment Form (Attachment 12)
requiredDocuments.description: Required if applicable to proposer's solution or approach

requiredDocuments.name: Cost Sheet (Attachment 13)
requiredDocuments.description: All-inclusive costs for software, implementation, maintenance, support, and training for 2 base years and 3 optional years; must be submitted as separate Volume 3 via separate Liquid Files submission

requiredVolumes: Volume 3: Response to Cost Requirements (must be separate from Volumes 1 & 2)

deliverables: Email marketing and campaign management platform (SaaS solution)
Implementation services including data migration, IP warming, testing, and platform setup
Security Plan (within 10 days of contract execution, updated annually)
Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plan
Ongoing customer support and dedicated account team

redFlags.whyItMatters: The solicitation requires minimum 5 years of experience across multiple mandatory qualifications (PQ1-PQ6, PQ12) including providing email platforms to large organizations, working with government entities, data modeling, and campaign advisory services. No relevant past performance was found for HORUSTECH in these areas. Failure to meet any mandatory qualification results in disqualification.
redFlags.mitigation: Conduct an honest bid/no-bid assessment. If HORUSTECH does not have 5+ years of direct email platform experience, consider whether teaming with an established email platform provider (as subcontractor) can satisfy the requirements, noting that the prime must demonstrate the experience.
redFlags.flag: No evidence of past performance in email marketing platform implementation or government email services

redFlags.whyItMatters: The solicitation states the Lottery currently uses Adobe Campaign Standard. Any proposer offering a competing platform must demonstrate migration capabilities, while Adobe (or its partners) would have inherent knowledge of the current data model, workflows, and integrations. The incumbent provider has institutional knowledge advantage.
redFlags.mitigation: During Q&A period (by January 8, 2026), ask detailed questions about current data architecture, workflow complexity, and integration points. Develop a thorough migration plan addressing data migration, IP warming, and workflow recreation.
redFlags.flag: Incumbent advantage — Lottery currently uses Adobe Campaign Standard

redFlags.whyItMatters: DQ17 awards 25 points for offering a dedicated, in-house implementation team (not third-party). This favors established email platform companies with their own professional services teams over resellers or smaller integrators.
redFlags.mitigation: If proposing as an integrator, ensure the platform vendor commits dedicated in-house resources. Clearly articulate in the proposal whether the implementation team is in-house to the platform provider.
redFlags.flag: Dedicated in-house implementation team is a desirable qualification worth 25 points

risks.whyItMatters: PQ1 requires at least 5 years of experience providing the proposed email service platform to organizations of similar size and scope to the California State Lottery (millions of registered players, 10M+ audience file, 1B+ annual sends). This is a significant barrier to entry for firms without an established email platform product.
risks.mitigation: Verify that the proposed platform vendor has documented 5+ years of experience with large-scale B2C organizations. If bidding as a reseller/implementer, ensure the platform provider can supply references meeting this requirement.
risks.flag: Mandatory 5 years experience providing email service platform to organizations of similar size and scope

risks.whyItMatters: PQ2 requires at least 5 years of experience working with governmental entities or large public sector organizations. HORUSTECH must demonstrate this experience through verifiable references with end-user contacts available during the evaluation period (approximately March 6-12, 2026).
risks.mitigation: Identify and confirm availability of government/public sector client references well in advance. Ensure references are responsive — failure to respond after two contact attempts can result in disqualification.
risks.flag: Mandatory 5 years experience with governmental entities or large public sector organizations

risks.whyItMatters: PQ7 and PQ8 mandate the platform handle at least 10 million audience records, over 1 billion annual email sends, and over 200 workflows/triggered automations. PQ9 requires unlimited storage. These are pass/fail requirements that eliminate platforms without enterprise-scale capabilities.
risks.mitigation: Only propose platforms with proven enterprise-scale capacity (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze, MessageGears, or similar). Obtain documentation from the platform vendor confirming these capabilities.
risks.flag: Platform must handle 10M+ audience file, 1B+ annual emails, and 200+ workflows

risks.whyItMatters: PQ12 requires at least 5 years of experience complying with FedRAMP, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM. FedRAMP authorization is a significant barrier — many email platforms do not hold FedRAMP authorization, which could limit platform choices.
risks.mitigation: Confirm the proposed platform holds FedRAMP authorization (or equivalent) and document 5+ years of compliance history. If the platform lacks FedRAMP, clarify with the Lottery during the Q&A period whether FedRAMP equivalency is acceptable.
risks.flag: FedRAMP, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM compliance required with 5 years experience

risks.whyItMatters: Section 3.11 requires background investigations including fingerprinting and FBI/DOJ inquiries for all staff working on the contract. Staff with criminal history may be disapproved, potentially disrupting the implementation team.
risks.mitigation: Pre-screen all proposed staff for potential background issues. Identify backup personnel in case any team members are disapproved by the Lottery.
risks.flag: Background investigations required for all contractor staff including FBI/DOJ fingerprinting

risks.whyItMatters: Section 4.3 states the Lottery may require demonstrations with only 7 calendar days notice, worth up to 200 points (20% of total). If demonstrations are conducted, failure to participate or perform well significantly impacts competitiveness.
risks.mitigation: Prepare demonstration materials and use-case scenarios in advance based on the SOW requirements. Ensure key technical staff are available during the demonstration window (approximately March 17-25, 2026).
risks.flag: Demonstrations may be required with only 7 calendar days advance notice

risks.whyItMatters: The NTR is worth 400 points (40% of total) but is limited to 14 pages total (4 pages each for Understanding & Approach, Implementation Services, and Customer Service Strategy, plus 2 pages for Assumptions). This requires highly concise yet comprehensive responses.
risks.mitigation: Begin drafting NTR sections early. Focus on specificity and direct alignment with evaluation criteria. Use tables and concise formatting to maximize information density within page limits.
risks.flag: Strict page limits on Narrative Technical Response (14 pages total, 4 pages per section)

risks.whyItMatters: Section 2.12 prohibits joint proposals. If HORUSTECH needs to partner with a platform vendor, it must structure the relationship as prime/subcontractor rather than a joint venture.
risks.mitigation: Structure any partnership as a prime/subcontractor arrangement with HORUSTECH as prime, ensuring subcontractor meets the 10% threshold disclosure requirements in Section 3.3.
risks.flag: Joint proposals not permitted — must bid as single prime contractor

incumbentInfo.notes: The solicitation states the Lottery currently uses Adobe Campaign Standard as their email platform, but does not name the contractor/implementer who holds the current contract. Adobe Campaign Standard is the product, not necessarily the contracting entity. No incumbent contractor company name is explicitly identified in the solicitation text.

data.confidenceExplanation: Confidence is 55 due to missing past performance data (mandatory reduction) but the NO_GO decision itself is extremely high confidence — the fundamental industry mismatch and expired deadline make this unambiguously non-viable regardless of any missing data.
requiredActions: Do NOT bid on this opportunity — it is fundamentally misaligned with company capabilities and the deadline has passed
Monitor future California State Lottery IT solicitations that align with cloud infrastructure, document automation, or AI/ML capabilities
If interested in state lottery work, pursue opportunities matching AWS consulting, data engineering, or AI/ML services
Consider subcontractor role to email platform vendors for future similar opportunities where AWS/data migration expertise could add value

criteria.name: TECHNICAL_FIT
criteria.rationale: Fundamental industry mismatch. Horus Technology is an AWS Advanced Consulting Partner specializing in intelligent document automation, cloud-native development, and AI/ML solutions. The solicitation requires an existing commercial email marketing and campaign management SaaS platform capable of 10M+ audience files, 1B+ annual email sends, and 200+ automated workflows. The company does not own, operate, or resell an email marketing platform product. The mandatory qualifications (PQ1-PQ15) require 5+ years of experience providing email platform services — capabilities completely absent from the company's documented portfolio.
gaps: No email marketing platform product or SaaS offering
No experience with enterprise-scale email delivery (10M+ contacts, 1B+ sends)
No email campaign management, segmentation, or automation capabilities
Cannot meet mandatory PQ1-PQ6 and PQ12 qualifications requiring 5+ years email platform experience
No data migration experience from Adobe Campaign Standard or similar platforms
No IP warming, email deliverability, or B2C email communications expertise
No FedRAMP-authorized email platform

criteria.name: PAST_PERFORMANCE_RELEVANCE
criteria.rationale: No past performance data available. The company has zero past performance projects in email marketing platforms, campaign management, or related services. The company's documented experience is in AWS cloud infrastructure, document automation, and AI/ML — a completely different domain from enterprise email marketing SaaS delivery.
gaps: No past performance in email marketing platform delivery
No government email services references
No B2C email communications experience
Cannot provide required 2-4 contract/implementations demonstrating relevant email platform experience within last 10 years

criteria.name: PRICING_POSITION
criteria.rationale: Pricing analysis confirms fundamental inability to compete. The company does not possess an email marketing platform product to price. Competitors will be established email platform vendors (Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze, Iterable, MessageGears) with economies of scale and existing product infrastructure. Price confidence is 12/100. The company's labor rates for software engineering are irrelevant since this requires a commercial SaaS product, not custom development.
gaps: No email platform product to price
Cannot compete against established SaaS vendors with existing products
No basis to estimate competitive pricing for a product the company does not possess
Fixed-price contract places all risk on contractor for non-existent platform

criteria.name: STRATEGIC_ALIGNMENT
criteria.rationale: This opportunity is in a completely different industry (enterprise email marketing SaaS) from the company's documented strategic focus (AWS consulting, document automation, AI/ML). No documented strategic plan indicates expansion into email marketing platforms. Pursuing this would be a distraction from core competencies with zero chance of success given mandatory disqualifying requirements.
gaps: Email marketing SaaS is outside company's documented strategic direction
No documented plan to enter email marketing platform market
Would require acquiring or building an entirely new product — not feasible within solicitation timeline
Company's AWS/AI/ML focus has no overlap with email platform delivery requirements

criteria.name: INCUMBENT_RISK
criteria.rationale: The current platform is Adobe Campaign Standard, giving Adobe or its implementation partners inherent knowledge advantage of existing data models, workflows, and integrations. While no specific incumbent contractor is named, any Adobe partner would have significant advantage. Additionally, established email platform vendors competing for this will have institutional credibility that Horus Technology completely lacks in this domain.
gaps: Adobe Campaign Standard incumbent knowledge advantage
Established email platform vendors will have strong competitive positioning
No relationship with California State Lottery in email services

data.summaryJustification: This is a clear NO_GO. Horus Technology is an AWS consulting firm specializing in document automation and AI/ML. The solicitation requires an established commercial email marketing SaaS platform vendor with 5+ years of proven experience at enterprise scale (10M+ contacts, 1B+ annual sends). The company does not own, operate, or resell any email marketing platform. Multiple mandatory qualifications (PQ1-PQ15) are disqualifying — the company would be eliminated at the Pass/Fail administrative review stage before any scoring occurs. Additionally, the proposal submission deadline of March 2, 2026 has already passed (today is April 19, 2026), making submission impossible.
blockers: Submission deadline has passed (March 2, 2026 — today is April 19, 2026)
Company does not own or operate an email marketing platform product — fundamental disqualifier
Cannot meet mandatory PQ1: 5+ years providing email service platform to organizations of similar size and scope
Cannot meet mandatory PQ2: 5+ years working with governmental entities on email platforms
Cannot meet mandatory PQ7/PQ8: Platform must handle 10M+ audience and 1B+ annual sends
Cannot provide required 2-4 contract/implementations demonstrating relevant email platform experience
data.decisionRationale: NO_GO decision based on: (1) Complete industry mismatch — company is an AWS/AI/ML consulting firm, not an email marketing platform vendor; (2) No past performance in email marketing or campaign management; (3) Cannot meet mandatory pass/fail qualifications (PQ1-PQ15) requiring 5+ years email platform experience, which would result in immediate disqualification; (4) Does not possess the required commercial SaaS product; (5) Submission deadline of March 2, 2026 has already passed — today is April 19, 2026. There is no viable path to compete for this opportunity.

confidenceDrivers.factor: No past performance data available in email marketing domain
Complete industry mismatch confirmed by KB (AWS/AI/ML vs email marketing SaaS)
Pricing analysis explicitly confirms company cannot perform this work
Submission deadline has passed (March 2, 2026 vs today April 19, 2026)
Mandatory qualifications are clearly disqualifying based on documented capabilities

data.summary: The California State Lottery is seeking proposals from qualified vendors to implement a replacement email marketing and campaign management platform, replacing their current Adobe Campaign Standard system. The new platform must support an audience file of at least 10 million, handle over a billion annual email sends, and provide robust segmentation, automation, personalization, analytics, and integration capabilities. The contract includes implementation services, data migration, ongoing customer support, and training for Lottery staff.
data.periodOfPerformance: 2-year base term + up to 3 one-year option extensions
data.title: Email Platform Replacement Services
